Package io.forja.components.dateAndTime.fxDatePicker

Class FxDatePicker

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.layout.Pane
javafx.scene.layout.VBox
io.forja.components.dateAndTime.fxDatePicker.FxDatePicker
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
public class FxDatePicker extends javafx.scene.layout.VBox
A styled date input with optional leading icon, min/max bounds, helper text, and validation-state variants. Wraps a JavaFX DatePicker.

FxDatePicker is a composite VBox of two rows:

  1. A framed HBox carrying the leading icon (if any) and the inner DatePicker (editor + calendar popup toggle).
  2. A FxLabel carrying the helper or error text — auto-hidden when both are empty.

The value is a bidirectional bridge to the inner picker's valueProperty. When setMin(java.time.LocalDate) / setMax(java.time.LocalDate) are set (inclusive), the calendar disables out-of-range cells; typed values outside the range are clamped on commit.

The display and parse format is controlled by setFormatPattern(java.lang.String) (default "yyyy-MM-dd"). Unparseable text reverts to the previous value on commit.

Setting setErrorText(java.lang.String) to a non-empty value auto-flips the variant to InputVariant.ERROR; clearing does not auto-revert.

Builder usage:

     
      FxDatePicker due = FxDatePicker.builder()
          .value(LocalDate.now())
          .min(LocalDate.now())
          .formatPattern("yyyy-MM-dd")
          .leadingIcon("fth-calendar")
          .helperText("Pick a due date.")
          .build();
See Also:
  • InputVariant
  • FxDatePicker.Builder

      Returns the value property. Holds null when empty.

      • setValue(LocalDate)

    • getValue

      public LocalDate getValue()
      Returns the current selected date, or null.

    • setValue

      public void setValue(LocalDate v)
      Sets the value; null clears. Clamped to [min, max].

      Returns the min property (inclusive, null = unbounded).

    • getFormatPattern

      public String getFormatPattern()
      Returns the current display/parse pattern.

    • setFormatPattern

      public void setFormatPattern(String pattern)
      Sets the pattern used for display and parsing (see DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern(String)). Default "yyyy-MM-dd".

      public String getHelperText()

      public void setHelperText(String v)
      Sets the helper text. Empty/null hides the helper line.
